title: Apply: plant reproductive success
description: Apply your understanding of plant reproductive success in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rat’s tail plants have tall stalks next to their flowers. Birds can perch on these stalks and feed on the flowers’ nectar.

[[☃ image 1]]

A scientist studied two groups of rat’s tail plants. One of the groups had their stalks removed. The other group had no change to their stalks. 

First, the scientist counted the number of times birds visited the plants to get nectar. Next, the scientist collected data about how many seeds each plant produced. The data is summarized in the table below.

  | Plants with stalks | Plants without stalks
- | -: | -:
Number of visits from birds | $24$ | $11$
Average number of seeds produced per plant | $125$ | $80$

**Which of these conclusions is best supported by the study described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A rat’s tail plant. The plant has a leaf, red flowers, and a brown stalk that sticks up beyond the leaf and the flowers.
options.caption: *A rat’s tail plant with its stalk labeled.*

choices.content: Rat’s tail plant stalks help with pollination.
Having a stalk leads to a decrease in the rat’s tail plant’s reproductive success.
Stalks are required in order for rat’s tail plants to reproduce.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rat’s tail plants have tall stalks next to their flowers. Birds can perch on these stalks and feed on the flowers’ nectar.

[[☃ image 1]]

A scientist studied two groups of rat’s tail plants. One of the groups had their stalks removed. The other group had no change to their stalks. 

First, the scientist counted the number of times birds visited the plants to get nectar. Next, the scientist collected data about how many seeds each plant produced. The data is summarized in the table below.

  | Plants with stalks | Plants without stalks
- | -: | -:
Number of visits from birds | $24$ | $11$
Average number of seeds produced per plant | $125$ | $80$
**Which of these conclusions is best supported by the study described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A rat’s tail plant. The plant has a leaf, red flowers, and a brown stalk that sticks up beyond the leaf and the flowers.
options.caption: *A rat’s tail plant with its stalk labeled.*

choices.content: Rat’s tail plant stalks help increase a plant’s reproductive success.
Having a stalk leads to a decrease in a rat’s tail plant’s chance of being pollinated by birds.
Rat’s tail plants can only reproduce if they do not have a stalk.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rat’s tail plants have tall stalks next to their flowers. Birds can perch on these stalks and feed on the flowers’ nectar.

[[☃ image 1]]

A scientist studied two groups of rat’s tail plants. One of the groups had their stalks removed. The other group had no change to their stalks. 

First, the scientist counted the number of times birds visited the plants to get nectar. Next, the scientist collected data about how many seeds each plant produced. The data is summarized in the table below.

  | Plants with stalks | Plants without stalks
- | -: | -:
Number of visits from birds | $24$ | $11$
Average number of seeds produced per plant | $125$ | $80$

**Which statement best summarizes the results of the study above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A rat’s tail plant. The plant has a leaf, red flowers, and a brown stalk that sticks up beyond the leaf and the flowers.
options.caption: *A rat’s tail plant with its stalk labeled.*

choices.content: Rat’s tail plants with stalks are more likely to be pollinated than plants without stalks.
Rat’s tail plants without stalks have a higher reproductive success than plants with stalks.
Having a stalk increases a plant’s chance of being pollinated, but it does not affect the plant’s reproductive success.

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below shows a seed caught in an animal’s fur. The seed is caught because of the thin barbs that stick out from the seed’s outer covering.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely function of the seed’s barbs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Barbed seed in fur](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Barbed_seed_in_fur_2159.jpg)" by Andrew Dunn, [CC BY-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A seed caught in an animal’s fur.

choices.content: to help with dispersal
to help with pollination
to help with fertilization

assessment_items.question_content:
Maple trees produce seed pods that have a wing-like structure, as shown in the image below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely function of the seed’s wing-like structure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[This seed of a maple seed runs as a helicopter](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:This_seed_of_a_maple_seed_runs_as_a_helicopter.jpg)" by Peter van der Sluijs, [CC BY-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A seed pod from a maple tree.

choices.content: to help seeds disperse via the wind
to help seeds fall more quickly to the ground
to help attract pollinators

assessment_items.question_content:
Coconuts are the seeds produced by a type of palm tree. These palm trees often grow on islands. The inside of a coconut is hollow, as shown in the image below. This hollow center allows the coconut to float.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely function of the coconut’s hollow center?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Coconut in half](https://www.flickr.com/photos/veganfeast/4492299082/)" by Vegan Feast Catering, [CC BY 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A coconut that has been cut in half, showing its hollow inside.

choices.content: to help the coconut disperse to distant areas
to help the coconut become pollinated
to help attract pollinators that like to eat coconut flesh

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below shows a bee orchid. This flower has specialized structures that look like the body and antennae of a bee. Male bees will land on the bee orchid, thinking it is a potential mate.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely function of the specialized structures described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Bee Orchid at College Lake](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bee_Orchid_at_College_Lake.jpg)" by The joy of all things, [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/deed.en)

choices.content: to attract bees that will pollinate the flower
to bring in bees that will help with seed dispersal
to increase the chance that a male bee will reproduce

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below shows a Rocky Mountain iris. This type of flower has stripes called nectar guides. These stripes help bumble bees find the flower’s nectar.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely function of the nectar guides described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A Rocky Mountain iris with stripes on its petals.

choices.content: to prevent bees from damaging the flower
to increase the plant’s chance of being pollinated
to help the plant’s seeds be dispersed

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below shows a corpse flower. Corpse flowers release chemicals that smell like rotting meat. This smell attracts flies and beetles that normally lay their eggs on rotting animal carcasses.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the most likely function of a smell like the one described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: to ensure that flies and beetles have a place to lay their eggs
to attract insects that will pollinate the flower
to bring in insects that will disperse the plant’s seeds

assessment_items.question_content:
Some poppy plants have flowers that reflect ultraviolet (UV) light. Scientists have found that plants that reflect UV light are easily seen by bees.

A scientist claims that the ability to reflect UV light increases a poppy plant’s reproductive success. 

**Which observation would best support the scientist’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Bees visit flowers that reflect UV light more often than those that don’t.
Plants with flowers that reflect UV light have fewer seeds than those that don’t.
Flowers that reflect UV light tend to have more petals than those that don’t.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dandelion seeds each have an umbrella-like structure at their top. This structure allows the seeds to be carried by the wind.

A scientist claims that this umbrella-like structure increases the dandelion plant’s reproductive success. 

**Which observation would best support the scientist’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Dispersed seeds are more likely than non-dispersed seeds to grow into new plants and survive.
Dispersed seeds grow into plants that produce less pollen than non-dispersed seeds.
Dispersed seeds attract more flying pollinators than non-dispersed seeds.

assessment_items.question_content:
Saguaro cacti have flowers that bloom only at night. These flowers release chemicals that smell like overripe melons. Bats are attracted to this scent, and they visit saguaro cactus flowers for their nectar.

A scientist claims that releasing a melon-like smell increases a saguaro cactus plant’s reproductive success. 

**Which observation would best support the scientist’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cactus plants that release a melon-like smell produce more seeds than plants that don’t.
Saguaro flowers that release a melon-like smell produce less pollen than flowers that don't.
Bats visit cactus flowers that release a melon-like smell less often than flowers that don’t.
